patch.value: pm health output stays large even with --brief/--skip flags; add a true one-line summary mode

Dogfood (2026-05-20): pm health emits ~1503 lines; --brief ~400 lines; --skip-integrity --skip-drift --skip-vectors ~1469 lines (skip flags cut compute time, not output size). For an agent that just needs ok:true/false plus failing-check names, this is heavy. The MCP pm_health brief projection helps but the CLI surface and brief size could shrink further. Proposal: a --summary mode returning just {ok, per-check name+status, warning_count} (a few lines), and ensure --skip-* also trims the corresponding output sections, not only the work.

A summary/one-line health mode returns ok + per-check status + warning_count in a few lines; --skip-* flags omit their sections from output; brief projection size measurably reduced; specs + 100% coverage green.
